nodes.id: 🔥 The Secret Is in the Flames
nodes.label: 🔥 The Secret Is in the Flames
nodes.title: 🔥 The Secret Is in the Flames

nodes.label: B001
nodes.title: Unusual spikes in outbound network traffic over port 443 may indicate unauthorized data exfiltration.

nodes.label: B002
nodes.title: AnyDesk Remote monitoring and management (RMM) tool not writing a file named "gcapi.dll" during installation may indicate a malicious version of AnyDesk was installed to establish persistence.

nodes.label: B003
nodes.title: Executables or scripts set in the rdpwd StartupPrograms registry key may indicate that an adversary has achieved persistence by setting a program to execute during an RDP login session.

nodes.label: B004
nodes.title: Identifying anomalous accounts may uncover adversary attempts to maintain persistence to compromised assets.

nodes.label: B005
nodes.title: Adversaries are exploiting the native Windows process Rundll32 in order to execute malicious code and bypass application control solutions.

nodes.label: B006
nodes.title: Adversaries are leveraging suspicious browser extensions to collect and exfiltrate sensitive data.

nodes.label: B007
nodes.title: Adversaries are automatically exfiltrating email data using email forwarding rules.

nodes.label: B008
nodes.title: Threat actors added an existing or newly created (B004) user to a privileged Active Directory (AD) security group to maintain persistence or achieve other objectives in an elevated context.

nodes.label: B009
nodes.title: Microsoft Playwright testing framework caches versions of various browsers for automated testing. In some version or configurations of this framework, the Firefox version is based on Firefox's Nightly

nodes.label: B010
nodes.title: Establish a normal behavior baseline for VPC peering across your AWS environment, so that future deviations (e.g., unauthorized peering, unusual traffic patterns) can be more easily hunted/detected. P

nodes.label: B011
nodes.title: Establish a normal utilization of Xcode and Xcode projects across workstations/servers. Xcode projects are commonly used by software developers and can be targeted by MacOS malware.

nodes.label: B012
nodes.title: Baseline all non-human identities associated with AI agents, automation frameworks, and agentic tools across the environment to identify orphaned service accounts from decommissioned agents that retai

nodes.label: B013
nodes.title: Baseline DNS query volumes, top queried domains, and resolution patterns across the environment to identify deviations that may indicate DNS tunneling, beaconing, or data exfiltration.

nodes.label: B014
nodes.title: Inventory and baseline all scheduled tasks, cron jobs, and recurring automation across endpoints and servers to detect unauthorized persistence mechanisms or task hijacking.

nodes.label: B015
nodes.title: Profile PowerShell usage across endpoints to establish normal execution patterns — including script paths, cmdlets, users, and execution policies — so that anomalous or malicious PowerShell activity c

nodes.label: B016
nodes.title: Baseline service account authentication patterns — including login times, source hosts, target resources, and protocols — to detect credential abuse or unauthorized use of service accounts for lateral

nodes.label: B017
nodes.title: Baseline TLS/SSL certificate inventory, issuance sources, validity periods, and renewal patterns across the environment to detect rogue certificates, expired certificates in production, or adversary-i

nodes.label: M001
nodes.title: A machine learning model can detect anomalies in user login patterns that indicate compromised accounts.

nodes.label: M002
nodes.title: Beaconing behavior can be detected in encrypted DNS traffic patterns by applying machine learning models that identify anomalous, periodic communication indicative of command and control activity.

nodes.label: M003
nodes.title: Machine learning models can identify anomalies with user or systems initiating outbound traffic with unusually large byte sizes that may indicate potential data exfiltration activity.

nodes.label: M004
nodes.title: Machine learning models can identify database query anomalies indicating potential data manipulation or exfiltration activity.

nodes.label: M005
nodes.title: Machine learning models can detect command-line obfuscation via Base64 encoding, which adversaries may use to evade detection.

nodes.label: M006
nodes.title: Dictionary-based DGAs are a rare threat that require a model-based approach. These domains are algorithmically generated based on a dictionary of source words. Like traditional Domain Generation Algor

nodes.label: M007
nodes.title: Compare text-based features of artifacts (User agent strings, Malware / Executables, Browser Extensions) by encoding them with a text-vectorizer. Vectorization creates a numerical representation of th

nodes.label: M008
nodes.title: Cluster process parent-child execution chains across the endpoint fleet to establish baseline lineage frequency, then identify rare or never-seen process trees that deviate from the norm to detect liv

nodes.label: M009
nodes.title: Apply time-series anomaly detection to authentication event volumes per user and service account to identify credential abuse, brute-force campaigns, and compromised accounts producing abnormal login

nodes.label: M010
nodes.title: Build a graph model of host-to-host authentication relationships and apply community detection and centrality analysis to identify anomalous lateral movement paths that deviate from established admini

nodes.label: M011
nodes.title: Use Shannon entropy analysis combined with n-gram frequency scoring on DNS query strings to detect domain generation algorithm (DGA) activity, DNS tunneling, and algorithmically generated subdomains u

nodes.label: M012
nodes.title: Apply text embedding similarity models to newly registered and recently observed domains to detect typosquatting, homoglyph attacks, and brand impersonation domains targeting the organization for cred

nodes.label: M013
nodes.title: Apply NLP classification and semantic analysis to email subjects, bodies, and metadata to detect business email compromise (BEC) attempts that evade keyword-based filters through social engineering la

nodes.label: M014
nodes.title: An adversary is deploying malicious npm packages with embedded MCPInject modules targeting developer environments to compromise AI coding assistants and exfiltrate LLM API keys, secrets, and cryptocur
